applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to forward method. Following AMD optimization guide recommendation to use torch.compile FIRST before manual kernels. The inductor backend automatically fuses ReLU+GroupNorm and generates optimized Triton kernels internally. Achieved 19% speedup over PyTorch baseline.

notes: Key learning: torch.compile(mode='default') on ROCm automatically fuses elementwise ops and outperforms manual Triton kernels for this workload
AMD optimization guide correctly states: 'torch.compile FIRST - gives 2-5x speedup. Get this working before any manual optimization'
Manual Triton kernels for GroupNorm were 13-117% slower than PyTorch due to: (1) two-pass algorithm overhead, (2) index computation cost, (3) register pressure
Final solution uses torch.compile which generates optimized Triton kernels via inductor backend
